sections.subtitle: Team Form and League Standing
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen, sitting at 2nd place in the league with 68 points, are in strong form as they host Borussia Dortmund at the BayArena in Leverkusen. Dortmund, positioned 5th with 51 points, are also on a good run with a streak of 4 wins and no losses in their last 5 matches. Leverkusen have been dominant at home, often scoring first in 5 of their last 7 games. Meanwhile, Dortmund have been prolific in attack, with 8 of their last 8 games featuring over 2.5 goals. Both teams have shown resilience, remaining unbeaten in their last 5 encounters against any opponent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, the results are evenly split with 1 win for Leverkusen, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Dortmund. Their most recent clash on January 10, 2025, saw Leverkusen triumph 3-2 away at Dortmund, with Patrik Schick named Player of the Match for his 7.9 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends indicate tight games, as 4 of the last 5 matches had under 2.5 goals. Both teams have scored in 8 of their last 10 encounters, suggesting we might see goals on both ends again. Leverkusen have not lost to Dortmund in their last 3 meetings, but they’ve failed to keep a clean sheet against them in 16 consecutive games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Leverkusen
sections.text: Leverkusen boast several top performers, including Patrik Schick, who ranks 2nd in the league for goals with 19 and has overperformed his expected goals by 7.6. Florian Wirtz is another standout, ranked 5th in average rating (7.68), 3rd in assists (10), and 3rd in successful dribbles per game (2.7). Defender Jonathan Tah impresses with a league 2nd rank for aerial duels won at 75.8%. Midfielder Alejandro Grimaldo also contributes significantly, ranking 3rd in key passes with 71. Additionally, Granit Xhaka’s playmaking with 7 assists could be crucial in breaking down Dortmund’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Dortmund
sections.text: Borussia Dortmund’s attack is led by Serhou Guirassy, who matches Schick with 19 goals, ranking 2nd in the league, and is in fine form with 7 goals in his last 5 matches. Pascal Groß, with 9 assists (4th in the league), is a creative force in midfield. Jamie Gittens ranks 4th in successful dribbles per game (2.6), while Julian Brandt’s 8 assists and 15 big chances created (5th in the league) highlight his importance. Defender Niklas Süle also stands out, ranking 3rd in aerial duels won at 70.6%, which could be vital against Leverkusen’s aerial threats.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen dominate possession with an average of 59.6% (2nd in the league) and excel in final third passes per game at 145.3 (also 2nd). Their pressing game is top-notch, ranking 1st in possessions won in the final third per game at 5.2. They’ve scored 68 goals this season (2nd in the league) but have underperformed their expected goals by 13.2. Dortmund, on the other hand, are lethal in the 16-30 minute period, scoring 24.5% of their goals then, while Leverkusen peak late with 27% of goals between 76-90 minutes. Both teams concede few big chances, with Leverkusen allowing the fewest in the league.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Referee Deniz Aytekin averages 3.9 yellow cards per game, and with both teams having a history of over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads, we can expect some cautions. Leverkusen have received 55 yellows this season (6th fewest), averaging 1.7 per game, while recent trends show under 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 home games. Corners are another area to watch, as Leverkusen average 6.3 per game (2nd in the league), but 5 of their last 5 home matches had under 10.5 corners. Dortmund’s games also tend to stay below 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 8 outings.

sections.subtitle: Venue and Historical Context
sections.text: The BayArena in Leverkusen will be buzzing for this clash between two Bundesliga heavyweights. Leverkusen’s home advantage could play a role, as they’ve been strong in scoring first in recent home games. Interestingly, Leverkusen have hit the woodwork 19 times this season, ranking 2nd for unluckiest in the league, which could either signal missed chances or an impending goal fest. Dortmund, despite their away form, have struggled to keep clean sheets against Leverkusen in their last 3 visits, adding intrigue to this matchup.
